activities.name: Nature Walk: Spot the Living
activities.description: Outdoor exploration where students identify living vs non-living things in the schoolyard. Students walk with clipboards and circle items they think are living.
activities.teacher_role: Guide the walk, ensure safety, prompt observations with questions
activities.student_role: Walk quietly, observe surroundings, discuss findings with partners

activities.name: Seed Sorting Adventure
activities.description: Students examine different seeds (beans, sunflower, pumpkin) using magnifying glasses, then sort them by size, shape, and color.
activities.teacher_role: Demonstrate how to observe carefully, help struggling students, facilitate group discussion
activities.student_role: Examine seeds closely, work in pairs to sort, share observations

activities.name: Growing Beans Time-Lapse
activities.description: Watch a time-lapse video of a bean seed sprouting and growing. Discuss what the seed needs to grow into a plant.
activities.teacher_role: Play video, pause to ask prediction questions, summarize key points
activities.student_role: Watch attentively, answer questions, share what they noticed

activities.name: Animal Classification Game
activities.description: Sort picture cards of animals into groups: animals that fly, swim, or walk/crawl. Introduce the idea that animals are living things with different features.
activities.teacher_role: Explain sorting rules, facilitate group sorting, introduce new vocabulary
activities.student_role: Work in groups to sort cards, explain their sorting choices

activities.name: Our Bodies: Living Machines
activities.description: Discuss how our bodies are living things that need food, water, and rest. Create a 'My Living Body' book with drawings of things their body can do.
activities.teacher_role: Lead discussion, give drawing prompts, help with writing names
activities.student_role: Draw and share what their body can do, discuss needs of living things

activities.name: Goldfish Observation Station
activities.description: Observe classroom goldfish (or fish in video) quietly. Students note how the fish moves, breathes, and responds to surroundings. Record observations.
activities.teacher_role: Set up observation rules, model quiet observation, guide note-taking
activities.student_role: Observe quietly, draw what they see, write one observation

activities.name: Living vs Non-Living Sort
activities.description: Whole-class activity sorting picture cards into 'Living' and 'Non-Living' categories. Discuss why things are living (grow, need food, move).
materials: Picture cards (rock, dog, sun, tree, car, fish, etc.)
activities.teacher_role: Lead discussion, introduce the 3 characteristics of living things, clarify misconceptions
activities.student_role: Place cards, explain reasoning, agree/disagree respectfully

activities.name: Plant Parts Investigation
activities.description: Examine real plants (grass, herb, flower). Identify and label roots, stem, leaves, and flowers. Touch and smell each part.
activities.teacher_role: Demonstrate safe handling, guide observation of each part, connect to plant needs
activities.student_role: Examine plants carefully, label parts, compare different plants
